evaluation.manager_summary: Isaac handled a friend-assisted intake call with excellent boundary management and empathy. He confirmed consent, collected substance use history (18-month cocaine use, alcohol for comedown, prior quit attempts), insurance details (Summit Select, member ID and group captured), and set a 90-minute callback with clear next steps. One minor coaching opportunity: avoid educational-clinical language about brain adaptation; defer mechanism explanations to clinical staff.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent acknowledged emotional state immediately, used caller's name, slowed down to match emotional register, and acknowledged difficulty of situation before moving to information gathering. The acknowledgment of Simone's fear and the supportive framing ('I'm glad you're on the line') was warm and genuine.

Agent collected carrier (Summit Select), member ID, and group number successfully. Framed verification as a step forward ('I'll verify your benefits'). Minor gap: did not explicitly confirm plan type, though this may have been deferred to the verification call.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Consider confirming plan type (HMO/PPO/etc.) during the initial collection to expedite verification.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent captured substance type (cocaine), duration (18 months), consequences (financial, lying), additional substance use (alcohol), and prior quit attempts. Screening was appropriate and stayed in intake role. Minor gap: did not explicitly ask about last use or withdrawal symptoms, which are severity indicators.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Ask about timing of last use and any withdrawal symptoms to help the clinical team prioritize urgency.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent set a 90-minute callback window, which suggests moderate urgency awareness. However, no explicit triage questions were asked (e.g., when was last use, current withdrawal, safety of living situation, suicidal ideation). The timeline is appropriate but not clearly grounded in assessed urgency.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Ask explicit urgency questions (last use, withdrawal symptoms, safety concerns) to ground the callback timeline in clinical need rather than operational routine.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent handled consent boundary immediately and clearly without being legalistic, confirmed Simone's comfort with Monica on the line, acknowledged Monica's supportive role, and correctly navigated between friend and patient throughout the call. Textbook execution.

Caller expressed fear/nervousness about making the call. Agent acknowledged it warmly ('That's completely okay'), normalized the emotion, gave permission not to talk, and provided reassurance grounded in the support system already present. No defensiveness, no rushing past the concern.

Agent clearly stated next step (admissions team callback), timing (within 90 minutes), ownership (our admissions team), and confirmed contact preference (include Monica). Caller was not left with any ambiguity about what happens next.

Agent deferred to clinical team for further assessment ('our admissions team'), and stayed in intake role. One borderline moment: 'it means your brain has adapted and needs support to reset' is educational-clinical language explaining a mechanism. It's brief and followed by appropriate deferral, but slightly crosses the line into explaining clinical pathways rather than purely gathering information.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Avoid explaining clinical mechanisms ('your brain has adapted') — instead, normalize the pattern and defer: 'That's common with stimulant use; our clinical team will help you understand what's happening and what support you need.'

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I'm glad she has someone like you to help her take this step.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: That's completely okay, Simone. You don't have to do any of the talking if you don't want to. I'm glad you're on the line.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Simone — having a friend willing to make this call with you is not a small thing. Hold onto that.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Yes. Summit Select. I have my card. Member ID BP34584961, group GRP-321429.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Thank you. Here's the plan: I'll verify your benefits and have our admissions team call you

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I've been using cocaine for about eighteen months. It's gotten out of control. I spend money I don't have. I've been lying to my family.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Simone, are you also drinking or using anything else alongside the cocaine?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay. And have you tried to stop on your own?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: You'll hear from us within ninety minutes.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I've been using cocaine for about eighteen months. It's gotten out of control.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I can share general information with you, but because Simone isn't a family member, I'd want to involve her directly before we discuss her specific situation in any detail.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Simone, can you hear me okay? I just want to make sure you're comfortable with your friend being part of this call.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I'm glad she has someone like you to help her take this step.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Yes. I'm here. I asked Monica to help me. I'm just really scared.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: That's completely okay, Simone. You don't have to do any of the talking if you don't want to. I'm glad you're on the line.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Simone — having a friend willing to make this call with you is not a small thing. Hold onto that.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I'll verify your benefits and have our admissions team call you — Simone, on your number directly, unless you'd prefer Monica to be included in that call too.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: You'll hear from us within ninety minutes.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Then I'll note that.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: it doesn't mean you lack willpower — it means your brain has adapted and needs support to reset.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: those are signs that this has moved beyond something you can manage on your own, and reaching out today is exactly the right move.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
